name: Aznalcóllar Green Corridor Loop
name_original: Río Crispinejo y Cota de los Frailes — circular desde Aznalcóllar
description: Hike the difficult 13.8-mile Río Crispinejo y Cota de los Frailes circular trail from Aznalcóllar, featuring unique semi-aquatic sections.
region_intro: Hiking around Aznalcóllar offers access to a diverse landscape characterized by green corridors, reservoir areas, and rolling terrain. The region features a mix of open spaces and paths that follow natural water features. These trails provide opportunities to explore the local environment, including the area around the Aznalcóllar reservoir. Best hiking trails around Aznalcóllar The most popular hiking route is Laguna de las Doblas — circular by Sanlúcar la Mayor, a 2.8 miles (4.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 9 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of the lagoon. Another top favourite among local hikers is Aznalcóllar Green Corridor Loop, a difficult 13.8 miles (22.3 km) path. This trail traverses the green corridor, featuring varied terrain and longer distances. Local hikers also love the Castillo de las Guardas – El Castillo de las Guardas loop from Bar Casa Juanito, a 8.9 miles (14.3 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 3 hours 57 minutes. Hiking around Aznalcóllar is defined by green corridors, reservoir areas, and rolling hills. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Aznalcóllar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 275 hikers have used komoot to explore Aznalcóllar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Aznalcóllar?
A: There are over a dozen hiking trails in the Aznalcóllar region. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options, allowing hikers to explore the diverse landscape of green corridors and reservoir areas.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Aznalcóllar offers several easy trails. A great option for a relaxed walk is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010568716/laguna-de-las-doblas-circular-by-sanlucar-la-mayor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de las Doblas — circular by Sanlúcar la Mayor&lt;/a&gt;. This 4.6 km (2.8 miles) route is generally completed in just over an hour and provides pleasant views of the lagoon, making it ideal for families.

Q: What kind of challenging hikes can I find in Aznalcóllar?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the region features more demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010832581/aznalcollar-green-corridor-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aznalcóllar Green Corridor Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 22.3 km (13.8 miles) path that traverses varied terrain and offers a longer, more strenuous experience through the green corridor.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Aznalcóllar are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21846020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de las Guardas – El Castillo de las Guardas loop from Bar Casa Juanito&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 14.3 km (8.9 miles) circular trail that takes you through the local countryside.

Q: What attractions or points of interest can I see while hiking?
A: The hiking trails often lead to interesting natural and man-made features. You can explore areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de las Doblas&lt;/a&gt; or witness the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6508564" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Overflow at the Dam&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3792314" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa of the Guadiamar&lt;/a&gt;, offering diverse landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Aznalcóllar?
A: The trails in Aznalcóllar are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from green corridors to reservoir views, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: Is it possible to hike near the Aznalcóllar reservoir?
A: Yes, the Aznalcóllar reservoir is a prominent feature of the local landscape, and several trails offer views or pass close to it. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27625525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Overflow at the Dam loop from Aznalcóllar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 12.5 km (7.8 miles) route that specifically explores the area around the dam and reservoir.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Aznalcóllar?
A: The best time for hiking in Aznalcóllar is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, sometimes wetter, conditions of winter. This allows for comfortable exploration of the region's diverse terrain.

Q: Are there any trails that feature unique natural elements like stone walls?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate distinctive natural and historical elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27625834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path Between Stone Walls – Scenic Rocky Path loop from Las Cañadillas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.2 km (8.8 miles) trail that highlights these unique features, offering a scenic journey through the landscape.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The Aznalcóllar region, with its green corridors and dehesa landscapes, is home to various wildlife. Hikers might spot birds of prey, waterfowl near the reservoir, and other small mammals. The natural environment provides habitats for a diverse range of species, especially in quieter areas away from main roads.

Q: How can I get to the hiking trails around Aznalcóllar using public transport?
A: While Aznalcóllar is accessible by public transport, direct connections to specific trailheads might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules to Aznalcóllar town. From there, some trailheads may be within walking distance, or you might consider a short taxi ride to reach more remote starting points.

Q: Where can I park my car when visiting the hiking trails?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Aznalcóllar town. For specific trailheads, look for designated parking areas or roadside parking where permitted. It's recommended to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for more precise parking information, as some popular trails may have small car parks.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Río Crispinejo y Cota de los Frailes trail?
faqs.answer: This trail offers a challenging and distinctive experience with rugged, abrupt, and steep reliefs. A significant portion follows the Río Crispinejo (also known as Río Agrio), which is semi-aquatic. You'll be walking and potentially swimming through various pools and stretches of the riverbed. The riverbed itself is notable for its unique yellow and ocher tones due to mining influences, and in some sections, the water can appear emerald green or turquoise, especially at spots like the Poza de las Palomillas. Expect a mix of dirt tracks and river sections.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The semi-aquatic nature of the Río Crispinejo section makes this trail especially suitable for summer. The water offers a refreshing and adventurous experience, which is ideal for warmer months. During other seasons, the water levels and temperatures might make the river sections more challenging or less enjoyable.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: This trail is located within the Corredor Verde del Guadiamar. While there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking this route, it's always advisable to check local regulations for the Corredor Verde del Guadiamar regarding access, especially for groups or specific activities. Generally, public access for hiking is permitted.

faqs.question: Is the Río Crispinejo y Cota de los Frailes trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficult classification and its significant semi-aquatic sections, including pools that can be deep, it might not be ideal for all dogs. While dogs are generally allowed in natural areas, consider your dog's swimming ability, fitness level, and comfort with rugged terrain and potentially acidic water before bringing them on this particular adventure. Always keep your dog under control and follow any local leash regulations.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending from Aznalcóllar. While specific parking details aren't provided, Aznalcóllar would be your base for accessing the trailhead. Look for public parking areas within the town or designated parking near the start of the Corredor Verde if available.

faqs.question: What unique natural features can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The most distinctive feature is the Río Crispinejo, also known as Río Agrio, with its unique yellow, ocher, and sometimes emerald green or turquoise waters, particularly at the 'spectacular' Poza de la Paloma (or Poza de las Palomillas). You'll also see landscapes shaped by mining history, views of the Embalse del Río Agrio, and dehesas with holm oaks and cork oaks. Keep an eye out for interesting bird species like the eagle owl, eagle, and black stork.

faqs.question: What highlights or points of interest are along the way?
faqs.answer: As you hike, you'll encounter several notable points. The route takes you through sections of the Green Corridor. You'll also pass by Cota de los Frailes and cross the Madera Bridge Río Agrio.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Corredor Verde, Cordel de los Carboneros, Camino del Tizón, and the Antigua via ferrea Aznalcóllar - Cama. It also briefly touches the Vía Verde de Itálica and the Cañada Real de la Isla o del Cincho.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike, especially considering the semi-aquatic sections?
faqs.answer: Given the difficult rating and the semi-aquatic nature, you should bring appropriate gear. This includes sturdy hiking shoes that can get wet or water shoes, swimwear, a waterproof bag for electronics and valuables, plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a first-aid kit. Trekking poles might be helpful for stability on uneven terrain and in the riverbed. A change of clothes for after the hike is also a good idea.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with young children?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is generally classified as 'difficult' or an 'expert hike' and requires a very good fitness level. The varied and demanding terrain, including walking and swimming through river sections with potentially deep pools, makes it unsuitable for beginners or families with young children. It's best reserved for experienced hikers comfortable with challenging conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near the trailhead in Aznalcóllar?
faqs.answer: Aznalcóllar is a town, so you should be able to find basic amenities such as cafes, pubs, and potentially some local accommodation options. It's a good idea to check in advance for specific establishments and their opening hours, especially if you plan to stay overnight or grab a meal before or after your hike.
